$note: A bound child/junction collection renders as a datalist of CARDS, never static containers. The HOST (dataContext v7 -> datalist v11) lives in the parent detail form; the ROW card template lives in a SEPARATE Table-type form referenced by datalist.formId.name (described here as $rowTemplate). CANONICAL LIVE EXAMPLES: view-requirement-card, view-endpoint-row, view-role-row (rows of view-definition-details-claude).  *** DATALIST ROW-TEMPLATE COLLAPSE/SCROLL FIX (read this) ***  In a row template every form component sits in an Ant form-item chain the framework forces to min-height:0, so multi-line text paints via overflow:visible OUT of a collapsed wrapper -> cards overlap, and the container's hard-coded inner overflow:auto shows scroll arrows. The fix is MARKUP-ONLY (no global CSS, no htmlRender) and is baked into this subtree: (1) the LEGACY `style` JS-string overflow on EVERY container -- 'return {overflow:\'visible\'}' on inner rows, 'hidden' on the card root -- the only channel that reaches & overrides the inner .sha-components-container-inner overflow:auto; (2) dimensions.minHeight '24px' + style whiteSpace:normal/wordBreak on the body text so it wraps to full height; (3) dimensions.minHeight:'fit-content' on containers; (4) refListStatus pills styled inline-flex height 26px; (5) header is a flex row with justifyContent:'space-between' (badges left, actions right). Do NOT use `customStyle` or the `overflow` boolean prop for this -- only the `style` channel works (verified live 2026-06-30). See capability-matrix 'datalist-row-template:multi-element card overflow/collapse'.

$slots.host.toolbar: subtree.components[0].components[0]  (quickSearch + pager strip; optional, omit for a bare list)
$slots.row.meta: $rowTemplate.components[2]  (optional meta row; omit if no meta)

$bindings: host.dataContext.entityType        -> {name, module} of the row entity (e.g. ViewRequirement / Shesha.RequirementsStudio)
host.dataContext.endpoint._code    -> GetAll filtered by the parent FK (e.g. parentView.id == data.id) + &properties= the row propertyNames
host.datalist.formId               -> {module, name} of the published row-template form (e.g. view-requirement-card)
row.numberBadge.content            -> sequence/refNo propertyName (e.g. {{data.refNo}}) — OMIT the badge if there is no stable per-row number (a datalist row template has no reliable index)
row.typeBadge.propertyName         -> the type reflist field (e.g. type)
row.typeBadge.referenceListId      -> the type reference list (e.g. RsRequirementType)
row.statusPill.propertyName        -> the status reflist field (e.g. status)
row.statusPill.referenceListId     -> the status reference list (e.g. RsStatus)
row.body.content                   -> the long-text propertyName (e.g. {{data.requirement}}); cap very long text in the content code if a denser card is wanted
$validatedAgainst: container:customStyle.flex(no-op for sizing -> width via dimensions; the space-between header + leftBadges maxWidth does the split, not flex)

$rowTemplate.$note: Published as its own Table-type form; the datalist stamps one instance per record. Root is the CARD surface (a styled container, NOT a 'card' component — the whole row is one flex column). The `style` overflow values and the body minHeight reserve are the row-template COLLAPSE/SCROLL fix (see block $note) and must NOT be stripped; brand visuals (bg/border/radius/shadow/padding/fonts) come from the requirement-datalist-row overlay.

content._code: $binding:rowNumberContent  // return data.refNo;  // OMIT this component if there is no stable per-row number

components.style: return { display: 'inline-flex', alignItems: 'center', height: '26px', overflow: 'visible', whiteSpace: 'nowrap' };
return { display: 'inline-flex', alignItems: 'center', height: '26px', overflow: 'visible', whiteSpace: 'nowrap' };
return { whiteSpace: 'normal', wordBreak: 'break-word', overflow: 'hidden', display: 'block', maxWidth: '100%' };
